name: Lokiz - The Caleras – circular from Galdeano
name_original: Lokiz - Las Caleras – circular desde Galdeano
region_intro: Hiking around Allín offers a variety of trails through the region's diverse landscape. The area features a mix of rolling hills, forested sections, and open terrain, providing varied hiking experiences. Elevation changes are present on many routes, contributing to panoramic views. The trails connect villages and natural points of interest, allowing for exploration of the local environment. Best hiking trails around Allín The most popular hiking route is Lokiz - The Caleras – circular from Galdeano, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is PuertoDeAramendia – Ibirin loop from Allín / Allin, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This circular trail explores varied terrain with notable elevation changes. Local hikers also love the MiradorDeLazkua – Altikogaña loop from Allín / Allin, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through natural surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Allín is defined by rolling hills, forested paths, and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to more challenging routes with elevation. The routes in Allín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Allín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Allín?
A: Allín offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 200 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy walks to more challenging paths, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Allín?
A: Yes, Allín has a good number of trails suitable for beginners and families. There are 45 easy routes available, many of which are part of the city's extensive greenbelt system, offering diverse experiences from unpaved nature walks to wider concrete paths. These trails are generally accessible and provide a great way to enjoy the outdoors.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Allín?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Allín are circular, offering convenient loop options. Popular choices include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970332420/lokiz-the-caleras-circular-from-galdeano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lokiz - The Caleras – circular from Galdeano&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain and views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969949470/penarroya-pena-negra-circular-desde-estella-lizarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya & Peña Negra - circular desde Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt;, exploring varied terrain. For a shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38289409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MiradorDeLazkua loop from Abárzuza / Abartzuza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Allín?
A: Hiking in Allín will take you through a mix of rolling hills, dense forested sections, and open terrain. The region is characterized by its greenbelt trails that follow creek corridors, offering natural beauty. You'll find diverse environments, from the natural preserve settings to paths that connect villages and local points of interest.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Allín dog-friendly?
A: Many of the trails in Allín, especially those within the greenbelt system and nature preserves, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and to check specific park or trail regulations before heading out to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes in Allín?
A: While hiking in Allín, you can explore various points of interest. For those interested in peaks, highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa – ConventoSantoDomingo loop from Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt; also passes by historical sites. The region's trails often connect to local villages and natural features, providing a rich exploration experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Allín?
A: The hiking experience in Allín is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to forested paths, and the well-maintained network of trails that offer options for all ability levels. The panoramic views from higher elevations are also a common highlight.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Allín?
A: Hike durations in Allín vary significantly depending on the route's distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970332420/lokiz-the-caleras-circular-from-galdeano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lokiz - The Caleras – circular from Galdeano&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 3 hours, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38289409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MiradorDeLazkua loop from Abárzuza / Abartzuza&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour and 11 minutes. There are options for quick strolls or longer day hikes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a unique cultural or historical experience?
A: Yes, some trails in the Allín area offer a glimpse into local history and culture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa – ConventoSantoDomingo loop from Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt; takes you past significant historical sites. Additionally, the Allen Station Trail, mentioned in regional research, features the Historic Water Station and Old Stone Dam, providing a unique historical context to your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Allín?
A: Allín offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. The extensive trail system, with its mix of paved and unpaved paths, means you can find suitable routes in most seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer can be enjoyed on shaded trails. Winter hiking is also possible, though some unpaved paths might be more challenging depending on conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for wildlife spotting?
A: The greenbelt trails and nature preserves in and around Allín, such as Oak Point Park and Nature Preserve, provide excellent opportunities for wildlife spotting. These natural corridors along creeks and through wooded areas are habitats for various local species, offering a chance to observe nature in a more secluded setting.

Q: What are the options for parking and public transport near the trailheads in Allín?
A: Given Allen, TX's well-developed infrastructure, many trailheads are accessible with parking facilities. The greenbelt trails are designed to connect to neighborhoods and parks, often making them easily reachable. For public transport, the city is part of a larger regional network, and while specific trailhead access might require local research, the overall connectivity is good.
tips: Small hermitage next to Galdeano, from the outside it looks more like a hut than a hermitage.
In a remote place in the beech forest and next to the top of Lokiz. I liked it a lot.
It is the peak that gives its name to the Sierra. Some other top has better views, but this one is also very pretty.
Rocky corridor to access the Sierra de Lokiz, it's nice to get to it and turn to the Sierra.

They are very very cool rocks, they reminded me of the menhirs that Obelix carried on his back! The route between the rocks is to be recommended, although the slope is hard and with a lot of loose stones.
